question:
Study the following information carefully and
answer the questions given below:
Twelve students are sitting in parallel rows. E, F,
G, H, I and J sit in row 1 while M, N, O, P, Q and R
sit in row 2. Students in row 1 and row 2 face each
other. Students of row 1 face south while
students of row 2 face north.
F sits second to the right of G, who faces O. One
person sits between O and Q, who sits at one of
the extreme ends. F does not sit at any extreme
end. M sits to the immediate right of N, who sits
fifth left to the Q. R sits opposite I, who is not
adjacent to F. I sits immediately right to the H. J
sits second to the right of E but not opposite to P.
How many students sit between M and Q in the
same row?
questionHtml:
Study the following information carefully and
answer the questions given below:

person sits between O and Q, who sits at one of

end. M sits to the immediate right of N, who sits
fifth left to the Q. R sits opposite I, who is not
adjacent to F. I sits immediately right to the H. J
sits second to the right of E but not opposite to P.

same row?
explanation:
1. F sits second to the right of G, who faces O.
2. One student sits between O and Q, who sits at
one of the extreme ends.
3. F does not sit at any extreme end.
So, we have one case,
4. M sits immediately to the right of N, who sits
fifth left to the Q.
5. R sits opposite I, who is not adjacent to F.
6. J sits second to the right of E but not opposite
to P.
7. I sits immediately right to the H.
So, we have,
According to the common solution, we can say
that three students sit between M and Q in the
same row.
Thus, the correct answer is option A.
